meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: It omits fertilizing contact between ovum and sperm, carrying only the onset of unspecified life inside a body part.
A.reason: A generic beginning of life inside a body does not distinguish conception from other processes that create living tissue or embryos.

B.missing: It replaces the actual formation of a new living zygote with the mere possibility that something can live after two small body parts touch.
B.reason: The two-body-part contact suggests egg and sperm, but possible subsequent life is insufficient to entail fertilization or pregnancy.

C.missing: It omits pregnancy, reproductive cells, and the creation of new life, carrying only generic action and contact between things.
C.reason: This skeleton applies to innumerable contact events and does not identify conception.

D.missing: It leaves the two contacting things unrelated to reproductive body parts and does not establish that the later living thing newly comes into existence.
D.reason: Contact followed by an unspecified living thing in an unspecified container is too generic to isolate fertilization.

E.missing: It omits bodies, ovum–sperm contact, and life, carrying only an unspecified action after which a new thing exists.
E.reason: The explication describes generic creation and fits countless nonreproductive events.

F.missing: It omits fertilization of an ovum by a spermatozoon, carrying instead only the beginning of a very small person's existence inside a body.
F.reason: It sharply renders the onset of embryonic life but also admits nonfertilization mechanisms such as cloning or parthenogenesis.
